plan_adherence_notes: Execution did not follow the plan. Relative to `ff/Tbayes-market-i6y-execute-step-4`, the branch diff contains only `.fleet/tasks/bayes-market-i6y-execute-step-4-execute-step-5/{questions,research,plan}.json`; there are no edits to `backend/server.py` or `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`. The current code still serves the legacy four-field `/positions` serializer from `serialize_account_position(...)`, the existing `BayesMarketExposureProjectionTests` still freeze that minimal contract, and repository search finds no `unrealizedPnl` seam or exact-P&L assertions. I ran `python3 -m pytest -v --tb=short tests/test_bayes_market_api.py::BayesMarketApiUnitTests::test_root_route_returns_service_index tests/test_bayes_market_api.py::BayesMarketExposureProjectionTests`, which passed with 15 tests, but that only confirms the old baseline remains green.
missed_questions: What exact rounding contract should `unrealizedPnl` use in the wire format, and should replayed average-cost / mark math round only the final scalar or intermediate values as well?
If `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(...)` cannot produce a mark for an otherwise live row, should `/positions` fail, omit the row, or fall back to `0.0` / `lastTradePrice`?
If replayed filled `EventTrade` history disagrees with the surviving live `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` row on net size or sign, which source is authoritative for `/positions`?
reason: Reject. The plan follows the research, but the branch never executes either the prerequisite replay/P&L production seam or the new exact-P&L `/positions` regressions. The focused pytest slice passes only because the branch is still on the legacy four-field contract, so this work is not approvable until the missing prerequisite is landed or the branch is rebased onto it.
